channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Melbourne boy trapped under train had bag snagged by mirror, school says
item.description: The St Aloysius College student remains in a serious condition in hospital after the incident yesterday afternoon.

media:group.media:description: Emergency services attend to the boy who was trapped under a train at North Melbourne Railway Station.

item.title: SA man forced to spend night in hospital ED due to lack of access cabs
item.description: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.
item.dc:creator: Josephine Lim

media:group.media:description: Jesse Frick spent a night at the Queen Elizabeth Hospital emergency room due to a lack of access taxis.

item.title: Four more ANU council members quit after Julie Bishop's shock resignation
item.description: The acting chancellor of the Australian National University confirms four more members of the university council have resigned, following last week's exit of former chancellor Julie Bishop.
item.dc:creator: Adam Shirley

media:group.media:description: The acting chancellor of the Australian National University in Canberra has confirmed four more members of the university council have resigned.

item.title: Tough 24 hours ahead for Keir Starmer as doubters call for resignation
item.description: UK cabinet will meet today, local time, amid growing calls for Keir Starmer to set out an exit plan.

media:group.media:description: Britain's Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer pauses as he delivers a speech, at the Coin Street Neighbourhood Centre in Waterloo, London, Monday May 11, 2026.

item.title: Racist attacks leave NZ's Indian community fearful
item.description: Indian communities across New Zealand say they are feeling "hounded" after a surge in racist incidents.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Costigan

media:group.media:description: Graffiti calling for violence against Indians out the front of Papatoetoe Central School in Auckland.

item.title: 'Leaks in the urinals': New exhibition explores media's role in political history
item.description: The new Press Gallery exhibition takes visitors inside the original working spaces at Old Parliament House where reporters worked between 1927 and 1988.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jennett

media:group.media:description: Malcolm Fraser leaves Parliament House after parliament was dissolved on November 11, 1975.

item.title: Live: Carlton boss addresses media after shock Voss resignation
item.description: Carlton CEO Graham Wright speaks to reporters after Michael Voss quit as Blues senior coach. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Michael Voss quit as Carlton's senior coach after a 1-8 start to the season.

item.title: New offences target 'kill cars' and recruitment of children by criminals
item.description: New offences and stronger punishments for the use of so-called kill cars and the recruitment of children by organised criminals will be introduced in the Parliament of NSW.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Corvo

media:group.media:description: The NSW government is introducing new legislation to tackle organised crime.

item.title: My four-year break from booze has led to an unexpected discovery
item.description: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Jones

media:group.media:description: Natalie feels like she has "shed a skin" to reveal her whole personality: braver and bolder.

item.title: Murder charge dropped against Melbourne man accused of killing Isla Bell
item.description: Melbourne man Marat Ganiev, who was accused of killing 19-year-old Isla Bell and putting her remains in a fridge, was due to face a Supreme Court trial this month.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Isla Bell's remains were discovered at a tip in November 2024, six weeks after she was last seen entering Marat Ganiev's apartment.

item.title: Six passengers from hantavirus cruise ship 'in good health', minister says
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of the deadly hantavirus outbreak are "in good health", as the Australian government remains hopeful it will finalise plans to repatriate them within 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Kate Christian and Courtney Withers

media:group.media:description: A passenger on board the cruise ship MV Hondius, affected by a hantavirus outbreak.

item.title: 'We are being attacked': Frantic call from a sailor to his family
item.description: As a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, low-wage seafarers are in limbo as they witness an unprecedented 21st-century battle.
item.dc:creator: Tavleen Singh and Som Patidar

media:group.media:description: The Epaminondas was seized by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Aussie female racing drivers star at home and abroad
item.description: Australia's strong cohort of young female racing drivers has shone at home and abroad, with a former F1 Academy driver achieving consecutive podiums on her Japanese debut and history made in the GT4 Australia.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Joanne Ciconte finished second in both races during round one of the 2026 Kyojo Cup.

item.title: Domestic violence charges against long-serving Qld mayor dropped
item.description: The long-serving mayor of Lockhart River on eastern Cape York was facing seven domestic violence charges but the case against him won't proceed.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: Wayne Butcher is no longer facing domestic violence allegations.

item.title: How Jess Pegula is rallying players in midst of French Open dispute
item.description: Jessica Pegula is putting her family experience of the NFL and NHL to use in tennis players' fight against the French Open for increased prize money.

media:group.media:description: Jessica Pegula is helping organise player action over the French Open pay dispute.

item.title: Police search for offender after man set on fire at apartment complex
item.description: Emergency service crews were called to an apartment complex in the Wollongong suburb of Warrawong overnight after a 25-year-old man was set on fire.
item.dc:creator: Penny Burfitt and Grace Crivellaro

media:group.media:description: Police were called to Todd Street in Warrawong last night after the incident.

item.title: Gout springs relay surprise as U20 world champs team named
item.description: Gout Gout is set to take part in the 200m and 4x400m relay at the Under-20 World Athletics Championships in August.

media:group.media:description: Gout Gout is part of a 75-strong team of Australian athletes.

item.title: Australian paratrooper killed after mid-air collision during training
item.description: Warrant Officer Class Two Lachlan Muddle, a highly qualified special forces sniper and military free-fall parachutist, has died after a mid-air collision with another paratrooper during an Army training exercise.
item.dc:creator: Penny Travers and Niki Burnside

media:group.media:description: Warrant Officer Class Two Lachlan Muddle, 50, was killed when he fell to the ground after a mid-air collision with another paratrooper.

item.title: Shot fired in alleged armoured truck heist in Queensland town
item.description: Police arrest a man who allegedly stole a significant amount of money from a cash-in-transit vehicle while armed with a gun in Hervey Bay.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Nunes-Malek

media:group.media:description: Police say the man allegedly stole from an armoured vehicle outside the Eli Waters shopping centre on Monday morning.

item.title: Deaths of young girls after Perth crash prompt charges against alleged driver
item.description: Police charge a 24-year-old man with dangerous driving causing death, a fortnight after two girls died from the injuries they suffered when their car smashed into a tree in Viveash.

media:group.media:description: Two girls aged three and four died in hospital nine days after their car crashed in Viveash.

item.title: Darwin company's $50 million plan to make diesel from gas condensate
item.description: Global Resource Recovery NT says production of the first diesel made from Central Australian gas condensate will leave Darwin in July.

media:group.media:description: A former biodiesel refinery is currently used as a chemical recycling facility in Darwin.

item.title: Captain of virus-hit ship praises all aboard as Australians set to return home
item.description: The MV Hondius's captain has praised the patience and kindness of his crew and guests as they disembark.

media:group.media:description: The MV Hondius has left Tenerife on its voyage to Rotterdam.

item.title: 'Relief' as Voss resigns as Carlton coach after torrid run of results
item.description: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Michael Voss coached the Blues in 103 AFL matches.

item.title: Hantavirus ship travellers headed to Perth's COVID 'white elephant'
item.description: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Hughes

media:group.media:description: The Bullsbrook quarantine facility is one of three Centres for National Resilience built during COVID.

item.title: Live: ASX slips as budget in focus while DroneShield probed by ASIC
item.description: Australian shares have slid while DroneShield is being investigated by ASIC over disclosures and share trading. Follow the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Lin Lin and Yiying Li

media:group.media:description: The ASX 200 is slightly up.
